mission.title: istio: unable to drain k8s node running Istio components with PDB
mission.description: unable to drain k8s node running Istio components with PDB. Requested by 27+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
Deployed istio 1.1.0 RC with helm chart (not template)

I would like to drain out a specific node because it needs a reboot. 
The PODs
```
Annotations:        scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/critical-pod:
```
prevents that.

steps.title: Apply the fix for unable to drain k8s node running Istio components with PDB
steps.description:
This is a proposal for "fixing": https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/12602

I based the logic on what i found in `gateways/istio-egress`.

This is just a proposal, another option could be to set `defaultPodDisruptionBudget.enabled = false` in `global.yaml` or update documentation to the effect
```yaml
Annotations:        scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/critical-pod:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "unable to drain k8s node running Istio components with PDB" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is a proposal for "fixing": https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/12602

I based the logic on what i found in `gateways/istio-egress`.

This is just a proposal, another option could be to set `defaultPodDisruptionBudget.enabled = false` in `global.yaml` or update documentation to the effect that default installs must be scaled before node maintenance is possible.
codeSnippets: error when evicting pod "istio-policy-d48655744-s8ggt" (will retry after 5s): Cannot evict pod as it would violate the pod's disruption budget.

$ kops rolling-update cluster --yes
...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
